page_content: ENEE446 Digital Computer Design (3 Credits) Hardware design of digital computers. Arithmetic and logic units, adders, multipliers and dividers. Floating-point arithmetic units. Bus and register structures. Control units, both hardwired and microprogrammed. Index registers, stacks, and other addressing schemes. Interrupts, DMA and interfacing. Prerequisite: ENEE350; and completion of all lower-division technical courses in the EE curriculum. Restriction: Permission of ENGR-Electrical & Computer Engineering department. Credit Only Granted for: ENEE446 or CMSC411.
